Name: ImageCAS
Strengths: Largest 3D coronary artery CCTA dataset; Public benchmark with implemented methods; Provides strong baseline model
Features: ECG-gated, Size: 512×512 , 0.25-0.45mm spacing, 128-slice dual-source scanner, adult patients

Name: ImageALCAPA
Strengths: First public 3D CTA dataset for ALCAPA segmentation; Designed to support 3D printing for surgical planning in a rare congenital condition.
Features: Structures: Myocardium (Myo), Left Ventricle (LV), Right Ventricle (RV), Pulmonary Artery (PA), Aorta (Ao), Left Coronary Artery (LCA), Right Coronary Artery (RCA). 265-slice scanner

Name: COCA
Strengths: Two complementary datasets (gated + non-gated); Includes both DICOM images and CAC segmentations + scores
Features: Gated dataset: DICOM images + coronary artery calcium segmentations (XML) + CAC scores; Non-gated dataset: DICOM images + CAC scores only
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: 3D CAS Images
Strengths: Voxel-level artery segmentation labels
Features: NIfTI (.nii) format; Each case: CT volume (.img.nii) + ground-truth segmentation mask (.label.nii); 200 3D volumetric cases; Voxel-level artery annotations

Name: MultiD4CAD
Strengths: First multimodal dataset combining CCTA imaging (EAT & PAT segmentations) with clinical risk factors for CAD prediction; Validated annotations by experienced radiologists
Features: 128-slice; ECG-gated acquisition; Slice thickness: 0.7mm, interval 0.4mm; Reconstruction: 10-20 phases (5-10% RR increments); Restricted FOV centered on heart; Segmentations: EAT & PAT (around IVA)

Name: ImageTBAD
Strengths: First 3D CTA dataset for TBAD with FLT annotation; Baseline method (3D U-net) provided
Features: 3 segmentation labels: True Lumen (TL), False Lumen (FL), False Lumen Thrombus (FLT); Multi-scanner ; Large variance in voxel size & resolution; Dual-radiologist annotation

Name: AVT (Aortic Vessel Tree)
Strengths: Multicenter dataset (3 sources: KiTS, RIDER, Dongyang Hospital); Covers full aortic tree from arch to iliac arteries; Includes pathological cases (AD, AAA)
Features: Ascending aorta, aortic arch + branches (brachiocephalic, left common carotid, left subclavian), thoracic aorta, abdominal aorta, iliac arteries

Name: AortaSeg-60
Strengths: Diverse pathology coverage (aneurysm, dissection, normal, elderly, young, venous); Fully automated segmentations via TotalSegmentator-AI; Most permissive license (CC0)
Features: 6 subsets: Anev (aneurysm), Dissec (dissection), Nat (normal), Old, Young, Ven (venous); CTA + non-contrast CT

Name: ImageTAAD
Strengths: First comprehensive TAAD segmentation dataset with 35 annotated categories; Includes clinically-oriented evaluation metrics (LOT, BVI, FLAR, TLC)
Features: 35 foreground categories including organs, true lumen, branch arteries, false lumen, tears; Size: 512×512; 256-slice scanners;

Name: HVSMR‑2.0
Strengths: Focus on Congenital Heart Disease (CHD); high anatomical variability
Features: 8 labels (LV, RV, LA, RA, AO, PA, SVC, IVC); includes "optional zones" for fair vessel evaluation

Name: ACDC
Strengths: Includes scribble annotations for weakly supervised learning
Features: Delineates LV, RV, and Myocardium; includes pathological cases

Name: CAMUS
Strengths: High clinical realism; Multi-structure annotations
Features: 4CH & 2CH views; EF labels; includes "poor quality" clinical cases

Name: EchoNet‑Dynamic
Strengths: Largest labeled medical video dataset; Expert annotations
Features: 112x112 resolution; Includes EF, ESV, EDV + expert tracings (ES/ED)
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: CardiacUDA (GraphEcho Dataset)
Task: Cardiac Structure Segmentation (LV, RV, LA, RA, PA) & Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA)
Dataset size: 992 videos (~102,796 frames) from ~100 patients across two clinical sites (Site G: 516 videos, Site R: 476 videos).
Strengths: First standardized benchmark for UDA in echocardiogram video segmentation; Multi-center data (two hospitals) ;
Features: 720p/1024p resolution, 100+ frames/video, pixel-level LV/RV/LA/RA/PA masks, 8:1:1 train/val/test split, Philips/HITACHI scanners, domain gap (Site G vs R)

Name: CHD68 Segmentation Dataset (MICCAI 2019)
Task: Whole Heart and Great Vessel Segmentation in Congenital Heart Disease
Strengths: First public dataset for whole heart and great vessel segmentation in CHD; Covers 14 types of congenital heart disease; Provides ground truth for 7 structures (LV, RV, LA, RA, myocardium, aorta, pulmonary artery);
Features: Size: 512×512; 64 -slice scanner; Patient age: 1 month to 21 years; 14 CHD types including ASD, VSD, ToF, TGA, etc.

Name: ImageCHD
Strengths: Covers 16 types of congenital heart disease; Includes both segmentation labels and classification labels
Features: Size: 512×512,  64-slice scanner; Patient age: 1 month to 40 years; 16 CHD types including ASD, VSD, ToF, TGA, DORV, etc.;

Name: EchoCP
Task: Patent Foramen Ovale (PFO) Diagnosis & Cardiac Chamber Segmentation
Dataset size: 60 videos from 30 patients (2 videos per patient: rest + Valsalva maneuver)
Strengths: First dataset for contrast TTE-based PFO diagnosis; Includes both rest and Valsalva maneuver states for each patient; Dual annotation (diagnosis + segmentation); Captures dynamic contrast flow patterns
Features: Apical-4-chamber view; ≥10 cardiac cycles per video; Valsalva maneuver performed 3-5 times during acquisition; Sparse labeling of segmentation (representative frames)

Name: EchoNet-LVH
Strengths: Large-scale parasternal long axis (PLAX) echo dataset; Expert sonographer measurements verified by level-3 echocardiographer; Enables both regression (wall thickness) and classification (LVH etiology: HCM, amyloidosis, HTN, AS)
Features: Parasternal long-axis view; Measurements: IVS, LVID, LVPW thickness at systole & diastole; Native resolution videos; Cropped/masked for de-identification
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: EchoNet-Pediatric
Strengths: First large-scale pediatric echo video dataset; Wide age/size range (0–18 yrs); Expert annotations (EF, LV tracings, measurements)
Features: Apical-4-chamber view; Labels: EF, ESV, EDV, LV endocardial tracings at ED & ES; Pediatric population incl. oncology, arrhythmia, heart failure, post-surgical, genetic conditions
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: EchoNet-TEE
Task: TEE view classification (8 standardized intraoperative views)
Strengths: First public dataset of clinically-acquired intraoperative TEE videos; External validation set for TEE view classification; Multi-procedure diversity (open cardiac surgery + transcatheter)
Features: 8 TEE view classes (ME 4-Chamber, ME Long Axis, TG LV SAX, ME AV SAX, ME 2-Chamber, ME Bicaval, ME LAA, Aortic); 112×112 px resolution; Includes color flow Doppler videos
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: Sunnybrook Cardiac MRI (SCD)
Task: Left ventricle segmentation (endocardium & epicardium); cardiac function quantification (EF, myocardial mass, stroke volume)
Strengths: First standardized benchmark for LV segmentation with open-source evaluation code; Covers 4 pathology groups (heart failure with/without dyssynchrony, hypertrophy, normal)
Features: Short-axis cine MRI; Expert endocardial & epicardial contours; 4 cardiac categories (normal, heart failure, LV hypertrophy, dyssynchrony); Clinical parameters (EF, MM, SV)

Name: 3C (Cardiac-CT-Covid19)
Task: Cardiac complication detection & classification in COVID-19 patients
Strengths: First CT dataset linking COVID-19 and cardiac complications; Wide age range (8-89 yrs); Dual-radiologist interpretation; Includes severity labels for each cardiac condition
Features: 2 groups: with cardiac complications (26 patients) vs without (108 patients); Binary classification label (cardiac complication: yes/no); Severity labels per cardiac condition

Name: OCMR
Task: Cardiac Cine MRI Reconstruction (accelerated k-space sampling)
Dataset size: 53 fully sampled and 212 prospectively undersampled cardiac cine series
Strengths: First open-access multi-coil k-space dataset for cardiac cine MRI; Includes both fully sampled (ground truth) and prospectively undersampled data; Multi-scanner diversity (1.5T & 3T)
Features: 265 HDF5 files (ISMRMRD format); 8 scan attributes (field strength, sampling pattern, echo, duration, view, slice type, FOV, subject); SAX & LAX views; patients + healthy volunteers

Name: Cardiac Atlas Project (CAP)
Task: Statistical shape modeling & functional atlas of the normal and pathological heart; LV segmentation
Strengths: Large-scale international multi-study database (MESA + DETERMINE); Combines imaging with clinical data; Open-source tools included; DICOM-compliant web-accessible platform
Features: Cine MRI (GRE & SSFP); Short-axis (6–12 slices) + 3 long-axis planes; 20–50 frames/cardiac cycle; 1–2mm pixel resolution; LV contours at ED & ES; Clinical metadata (age, gender, BP, heart rate, pathology)

Name: HOCMvalvesSeg
Task: Segmentation of Aortic/Mitral Valves and Heart Structures
Strengths: First public dataset focused on valve segmentation for Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy (HOCM) surgical planning; Supports 3D modeling for septal myectomy
Features: Structures: Aortic Valve (AV), Mitral Valve (MV), Aorta (AO), Left Atrium (LA), Left Ventricle (LV), Myocardium, Excised Myocardium. 256-slice scanner. Size: 512×512, Patient age: 38-76 yrs

Name: RSNA Pulmonary Embolism CT Dataset (RSPECT)
Strengths: Largest publicly available expert-annotated CTPA dataset; Multinational (5 countries); Annotated by 86 subspecialist thoracic radiologists; Rich study-level labels beyond binary PE detection
Features: Study-level labels: Central/Right/Left PE, Acute/Chronic, RV/LV ratio, flow artifact, QA; Image-level label: PE Present on Image; Section thickness: 2.5–3.0 mm; 5 institutions (US, Canada, Brazil, Australia, Turkey)

Name: Radfusion
Strengths: First public dataset combining 3D CT with longitudinal EHR for PE; First fairness evaluation of multimodal fusion models; Expert annotations
Features: 1.25mm axial CT slices; 600,000+ 2D slices; Window: center=400, width=1000 (pulmonary artery optimized); Median 386 slices/study; PE labels: Central, Segmental, Subsegmental
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: FUMPE
Strengths: Dual expert radiologist annotations; Published in Scientific Data (Nature); One of the earliest public PE datasets with pixel-level ground truth
Features: CTA images + ground truth masks; Semi-automated annotation tool; 35 patients

Name: INSPECT
Strengths: First dataset linking CTPA images + radiology reports + full longitudinal EHR for PE patients; Largest PE multimodal dataset publicly available
Features: 3 modalities: CTPA scans, radiology reports (unstructured), EHR (9 OMOP tables); Prognosis tasks: 3; Diagnosis tasks: 1; Stanford Hospital data; STARR OMOP format
License: Stanford University Dataset Research Use Agreement

Name: ChestPathCT5-S100
Task: Thoracic pathology classification (PE, rib fracture, pleural fluid, pulmonary mass, pneumothorax)
Strengths: Multi-pathology chest CT dataset from clinical practice; Includes both contrast and non-contrast scans
Features: NIfTI (.nii.gz) format; 5 pathology categories; Adult patients; Contrast + non-contrast; No segmentation masks, raw scans only

Name: ImageVessel
Strengths: First public dataset for pulmonary artery & vein segmentation to aid lung cancer surgery planning; Annotations created and validated in clinical practice by expert surgeons; Supports development of 3D visualization (VR/3D printing) for surgery.
Features: Size: 512×512;  Patient age: 29-82 yrs; Annotations: Intrapulmonary & extrapulmonary arteries/veins + lung areas; Focus: Half-lung annotations per scan

Name: CUBS (Carotid Ultrasound Boundary Study)
Strengths: Open multicenter benchmark (multiple centers); Compares 7 automated CIMT algorithms (traditional + deep learning) vs. 3 expert manual segmentations; Stratified analysis by morphology, SNR, and center
Features: Longitudinal B-mode common carotid artery images; 3 manual segmentations + 7 computerized segmentations per image; Conversion factors included

Name: Common Carotid Artery Ultrasound Images
Strengths: Includes expert segmentation masks (technician + expert verification); Time series converted from DICOM; Simple and accessible format
Features: B-mode carotid US (carotid & vascular modalities); 709×749×3 px PNG images; 2200 files total (images + masks); Mindray UMT-500Plus, L13-3s linear probe; 100 images/subject

Name: KiPA22
Strengths: Includes fine renal vascular structure annotations (artery + vein); Contains abnormal kidney samples (tumors); Only public dataset with annotated renal artery/vein segmentation
Features: 4 classes: kidney, kidney tumor, renal artery, renal vein; Isotropic spacing ~0.63mm; Size ~150×150×198 voxels; NIfTI (.nii.gz); Kidney-cropped volumes

Name: TotalSegmentator CT
Strengths: Largest public multi-organ CT segmentation dataset; Covers majority of cardiovascular structures; Trained on highly diverse real-world clinical data
Features: Structures: 117 classes. Key cardiovascular structures: Aorta, Pulmonary Artery, Heart Chambers (Left/Right Atrium & Ventricle, Myocardium), Iliac Arteries/Veins, Inferior Vena Cava, Portal & Splenic Veins

Name: Medical Segmentation Decathlon (MSD)
Task: Multi-organ segmentation across 10 tasks (brain, heart/left atrium, hippocampus, liver, lung, pancreas, prostate, colon, hepatic vessels, spleen)
Strengths: First challenge to test algorithmic generalizability across multiple tasks and modalities simultaneously; Largest and most comprehensive multi-task medical segmentation benchmark
Features: 10 tasks, 17 target ROIs; Multi-institution, multi-modality (CT + MRI); NIfTI format

Name: TotalSegmentator MRI
Strengths: Largest public multi-organ MRI segmentation dataset; Covers majority of cardiovascular structures; Real-world clinical data from routine practice; Includes IDC images for additional diversity
Features: Structures: 50 classes. Key cardiovascular: Heart, Aorta, Inferior Vena Cava, Portal & Splenic Veins, Iliac Arteries/Veins

Name: AortaSeg24
Strengths: First dataset with 23 clinically relevant aortic branches and SVS/STS zones annotated; Expert vascular surgeon annotations; Public benchmark with top-performing implementations available
Features: 23 classes: aortic zones 0–11 + branches (innominate, carotid, subclavian, celiac, SMA, renal, iliac arteries); 512×512×245–962 slices; Resampled to 1×1×1 mm isotropic; uTBAD patients only
